title: 10 best herbal creams for viral hepatitis

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, can reduce liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis, alleviating symptoms like pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's high antioxidant content helps protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate liver disease progression in patients with viral hepatitis.

Choleretic properties: Dandelion root's choleretic effect, triggered by the presence of inulin and other saponins, can stimulate bile production and flow, helping to eliminate toxins and reduce liver congestion associated with viral hepatitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with viral hepatitis because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's flavonoids, such as taraxasterol and inulin, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are key factors in the progression of viral hepatitis. Additionally, dandelion's bioactive compounds may help to modulate the immune system, promoting a more balanced response to the virus and supporting the liver's natural healing processes. This may contribute to improved liver function and overall health.
remedy_constituents: Apolipoprotein A-I: Helps with viral hepatitis by reducing liver inflammation and promoting the elimination of hepatitis viruses through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Taraxasterol: Assists in alleviating viral hepatitis symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to the reduction of liver damage and the improvement of liver function.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Used for their diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 20 freshly dried taraxacum officinale leaves in a clean container for storage purposes.
Steep 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves in 200 milliliters of distilled water for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix 1 gram of beeswax and 2 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the strained liquid with the melted beeswax mixture in a 1:1 ratio to create the taraxacum officinale cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has fallen when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Infuse 200 grams of dried silybum marianum in 1 liter of carrier oil such as jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth to separate the plant material from the oil.
Mix 100 grams of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Mix 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 20 grams of sweet almond oil and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 20 minutes while gently stirring until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Strain the mixture into a clean glass container and allow it to cool completely before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves and flowers of artemisia absinthium at the peak of their potency when in full bloom within 6 hours.

Infuse 25 grams of dried artemisia absinthium in 250 milliliters of high-quality coconut oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the plant material using cheesecloth or a coffee filter within 30 minutes.
Blend 100 grams of shea butter with 50 grams of the infused oil and 10 grams of beeswax to create a smooth cream within 10 minutes.

Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for six hours then whip it into a smooth creamy consistency using an electric mixer or whisk.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream helps reduce inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms and slow down liver damage caused by viral hepatitis.

Antiviral properties: Nettle's antiviral compounds, such as apigenin and quercetin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of certain viruses that cause hepatitis, including hepatitis A, B, and C.
Antioxidant properties: Stinging nettle cream's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall liver health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the stinging nettle plant are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing skin irritations associated with viral hepatitis.

Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, helping to combat the virus and reduce symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry urtica dioica leaves according to conventional methods.
Combine one part dried urtica dioica leaves with two parts coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the oil is infused with the herb's active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and storing the liquid.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for several hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with viral hepatitis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help to soothe and calm the liver and surrounding tissues. The antimicrobial properties of elder extract also aid in combating the viral infection, while its antiviral activity helps to inhibit the replication of the virus, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting recovery. Additionally, elder creams may also improve liver function by enhancing detoxification processes, leading to improved overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by viral hepatitis, thereby slowing down liver damage and promoting recovery.

Immunomodulatory effects: The flavonoids present in elderberry have been shown to modulate the immune response, enhancing the body's natural defenses against viral infections like hepatitis, and reducing the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anthocyanins, help reduce inflammation and liver damage caused by viral hepatitis, thereby improving overall health outcomes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver damage caused by viral hepatitis.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique combination of triterpenoid saponins, rhyzomes have been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses, making them effective in combating viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried sambucus nigra flowers and organic beeswax in a clean environment.
Combine one part of dried sambucus nigra flowers with two parts of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and mix it with 20% beeswax by weight to create the cream base.
Whip the mixture thoroughly until smooth, then transfer it to sterile jars and store them in a cool place.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for viral hepatitis are topical remedies that help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process for viral hepatitis, a liver disease caused by viral infections. The best herbal creams for viral hepatitis include Curcuma longa, Silybum marianum, Phyllanthus niruri, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Andrographis paniculata, Picrorhiza kurroa, Tribulus terrestris, Terminalia chebula, and Withania somnifera. If left untreated, viral hepatitis can lead to severe liver damage, fatigue, and a weakened immune system, negatively impacting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to viral hepatitis that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing symptoms of redness, itching, and inflammation. These natural remedies can provide relief from skin irritation and promote healthy skin regeneration without harsh chemicals or side effects.
supplementary_best_treatment: I can't answer that. If you are experiencing symptoms of viral hepatitis, I recommend that you seek advice from a qualified healthcare professional or registered herbalist.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with viral hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, the active compound in Curcuma longa, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis. By inhibiting the replication of the hepatitis virus, curcumin helps to slow down the progression of the disease. Additionally, curcumin has been found to enhance the immune system's ability to fight off the virus, leading to improved liver function and overall health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It possesses potent antiviral properties that can inhibit the replication of hepatitis B and C viruses, thereby reducing liver damage.
Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has been shown to exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities that can help alleviate liver inflammation and fibrosis caused by viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high content of curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis.
Roots: Roots are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve overall liver function in individuals with viral hepatitis.
Stems: Stems are used due to their richness in bioactive compounds that may help modulate the immune system and reduce oxidative stress in individuals with viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried turmeric roots in 200ml of carrier oil at 70 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids after 1 hour.
Mix 5% w/w of beeswax with 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Blend the beeswax mixture with the infused oil at 40 degrees celsius for 5 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into sterile jars and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with viral hepatitis because they contain silymarin, a bioactive compound that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Silymarin helps to protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, which can exacerbate the condition. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, silymarin can improve liver function and promote healing in individuals with viral hepatitis, potentially slowing disease progression and improving overall health. This natural compound has been shown to be effective in supporting liver health.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: A polyphenolic complex with antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce liver inflammation and protect liver cells from damage caused by viral hepatitis.
Silibinin: A major component of silymarin with antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibits viral replication and reduces liver fibrosis associated with viral hepatitis.
Isosilybin: A flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce liver inflammation and scarring, and promotes liver regeneration in cases of viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: These are the primary part used due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties which help in treating viral hepatitis.
Leaves: Leaves are used in some creams due to their ability to absorb and reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with viral hepatitis.

Flowers: Although less commonly used than seeds, flowers are sometimes used in creams for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties which can aid in the treatment of viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50g of silybum marianum flowers and leaves and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or blender.
Mix 10g of the silybum marianum powder with 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 10 minutes until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus niruri creams helps with viral hepatitis because they contain flavonoids and tannins, which have been shown to exhibit potent antiviral properties. The cream's active compounds, such as phyllanthin and hypophyllanthin, have been found to inhibit the replication of hepatitis B virus, helping to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. By reducing viral load and oxidative stress, Phyllanthus niruri creams may help alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis and support the body's natural recovery process. This natural remedy may offer a complementary approach to conventional treatment.
remedy_constituents: Phyllanthin: This lignan exhibits potent antiviral properties, inhibiting the replication of hepatitis B virus (HBV) and reducing viral load in infected cells.

Niruri flavonoids: These flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help mitigate liver damage and inflammation caused by viral hepatitis, promoting liver health and recovery.

Corilagin: This tannin has been shown to inhibit the replication of HBV and exhibit immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defenses against viral infections and supporting liver function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are believed to exhibit antiviral properties.
Stems: Utilized for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral activities, which can help in managing viral hepatitis symptoms.
Seeds: Employed for their bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and lignans, which have been shown to have antiviral and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh phyllanthus niruri leaves from a well-maintained garden or a trusted supplier.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on a paper towel or cloth for 24 hours.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried phyllanthus niruri leaves in 2 tablespoons of coconut oil for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with viral hepatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis. It also promotes liver regeneration and improves liver function by increasing glutathione levels and reducing lipid peroxidation. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has antiviral properties that can inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, making it a potential adjunctive therapy for managing viral hepatitis. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra creams a promising natural remedy for viral hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Licopyranocoumarin glycoside: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative damage associated with viral hepatitis.
Glycyrrhizin: This constituent has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce viral replication and inflammation in the liver caused by viral hepatitis.
Flavonoids: This class of constituents has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver caused by viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound with antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating various health conditions.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which helps in reducing inflammation and fighting viral infections.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help in treating various health conditions, including viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered roots with 20 grams of jojoba oil in a double boiler and heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled to 40 degrees celsius.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with viral hepatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function. Additionally, ginger has been found to have antiviral properties, which can help combat the hepatitis virus. By reducing inflammation and fighting the virus, Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process for individuals suffering from viral hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress caused by viral hepatitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Shogaols: Inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses and reduce liver damage by exhibiting antiviral and hepatoprotective properties.
Curcuminoids: Reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress caused by viral hepatitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis.
Roots: They are used because of their rich content of curcuminoids and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antioxidant and immune-modulating effects beneficial for treating viral hepatitis.
Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other polyphenols that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help manage symptoms of viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizome in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean container.
Combine 200 grams of distilled water with 100 grams of glycerin in a double boiler.
Gradually add the infused zingiber officinale liquid to the water and glycerin mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before pouring into containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata creams helps with viral hepatitis because it contains a unique compound called andrographolide, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds work together to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, helping to alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis. The cream's active ingredients also boost the immune system, enabling the body to fight off the viral infection more effectively, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of long-term liver damage.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: This diterpenoid lactone helps to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to its hepatoprotective effects against viral hepatitis.
Andrographiside: This diterpenoid lactone derivative has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against hepatitis viruses, thereby helping to reduce viral replication and liver damage.
Neohesperidin: This flavonoid glycoside has been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to mitigate liver damage and inflammation caused by viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain andrographolide, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis.
Rhyzomes: They contain a variety of compounds, including andrographolide, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis.
Roots: They contain andrographolide and other bioactive compounds that may help modulate the immune system and reduce liver damage caused by viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Extract 10-15 grams of dried andrographis paniculata leaves using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a 1:2 ratio.
Strain the extract through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container within 30 minutes.
Mix the extract with a base oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:5 ratio within 20 minutes.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture within 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 2-3 minutes to achieve a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Picrorhiza kurroa creams helps with viral hepatitis because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including kutkin and picroliv. These compounds possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm liver inflammation caused by viral hepatitis. The antiviral properties of Picrorhiza kurroa creams also help to suppress the replication of the virus, thereby reducing the severity of the disease. Its hepatoprotective effects further aid in promoting liver health and regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds exhibit antiviral properties, helping to inhibit the replication of hepatitis-causing viruses and alleviate symptoms associated with viral hepatitis.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Picrorhiza kurroa possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help reduce liver inflammation and improve the body's immune response to viral hepatitis.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate oxidative stress and liver damage caused by viral hepatitis, ultimately promoting liver health and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, specifically picrorhizin and picroliv, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of base cream and 50 grams of picrorhiza kurroa powder in a mixing bowl.
Dissolve 50 grams of glycerin and 25 grams of distilled water in the base cream.
Mix 10 grams of xanthan gum with 25 grams of distilled water to create a thickening solution.
Add the xanthan gum solution to the base cream mixture and blend at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a stainless steel container and allow it to set for 24 hours at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Tribulus terrestris creams help with viral hepatitis because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing liver inflammation and damage. The extract of Tribulus terrestris contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to improve liver function, boost the immune system, and reduce oxidative stress. This natural remedy may also help to regenerate liver cells, promote liver detoxification, and support the body's natural defenses against viral hepatitis. As a result, it may help alleviate symptoms and support overall liver health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which help inhibit the replication of hepatitis viruses and reduce liver inflammation.

Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the liver from oxidative damage, reduce liver inflammation, and improve liver function in patients with viral hepatitis.

Furostanol saponins: These saponins have been found to have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system's response to viral hepatitis, reduce viral load, and promote liver regeneration.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds that may have antiviral properties.
Leaves: Used due to their ability to modulate the immune system and potentially reduce liver inflammation.
Roots: Used due to their high content of alkaloids and glycosides that may have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of tribulus terrestris roots and leaves from a clean and dry environment.
Dry the harvested plant material at 30-40°c for 2-3 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried tribulus terrestris into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered tribulus terrestris with 8 ounces of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture has cooled to a solid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Terminalia chebula creams helps with viral hepatitis because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver. The herb contains chebulic acid, which has been shown to have antiviral properties, inhibiting the replication of viral particles and promoting the clearance of viral antigens from the liver. Additionally, Terminalia chebula has been found to enhance liver function and stimulate the production of liver enzymes, aiding in the recovery of liver cells and tissue damaged by viral hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Chebulic acid: This triterpenoid saponin exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis.

Gallic acid: A phenolic acid with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, gallic acid may help protect liver cells from oxidative damage and promote their recovery in the context of viral hepatitis.

Chebulinic acid: This triterpenoid saponin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce liver damage in viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used to make creams for viral hepatitis because they contain chebulic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Barks: They are used to make creams for viral hepatitis because they contain chebulinic acid, which has been found to have hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.

Seeds: They are used to make creams for viral hepatitis because they contain chebulagic acid, which has been demonstrated to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to protect the liver from damage.
remedy_recipe: Crush 5 grams of terminalia chebula powder in a mortar using a pestle until it becomes fine.
Mix the crushed powder with 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it is fully melted and combined.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 1 hour.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it becomes light and fluffy and has a smooth texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams helps with viral hepatitis because they contain the herb Ashwagandha, which has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to enhance liver function and reduce inflammation. The withanolides present in Ashwagandha have been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, Withania somnifera creams may help to alleviate symptoms of viral hepatitis and promote liver health, supporting the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with viral hepatitis.
Withaferin A: This withanolide has been shown to have antiviral properties, inhibiting the replication of viral particles and reducing liver damage caused by viral hepatitis.

Alkaloids: These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration in the context of viral hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of withanolides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: They are used because they contain antioxidants and other compounds that may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with viral hepatitis.
Fruits: They are used due to their potential in reducing liver damage and promoting liver regeneration, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100 grams of withania somnifera roots by macerating them in 500ml of glycerin for 2 weeks.
Filter the mixture using a cheesecloth to remove the plant material and obtain a liquid extract.
Combine the filtered liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under gentle stirring for 30 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil melt and mix.
Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool and solidify completely before use.
